channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Follow the steps to deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Configure Update Policies for Apple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure software update policies for Apple devices using Apple's Declarative Device Management (DDM) model. Improve security, reduce user disruption, and ensure compliance with reliable, automated updates across your organization.

item.title: Licenses available for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune is available with these licenses

item.title: Device Query for Multiple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use device query for multiple devices in Microsoft Intune to run KQL queries, analyze inventory trends, and start querying your managed fleet now.

item.title: Configure Endpoint Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step-by-step guide to configure endpoint analytics in Microsoft Intune, enroll devices, and manage data collection policies for device insights.

item.title: Create a policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure thousands of settings for Windows 10/11, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android client devices, including Microsoft Office apps, Microsoft Edge, administrative templates (ADMX), and more. Add these settings in a device configuration profile to secure devices, and control different programs and features. Use Microsoft Copilot to get What If analysis, and learn more about each setting.

item.title: Manage and secure apps overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing apps that access organization resources in Microsoft Intune. You can deploy apps used by your organization, including Microsoft Edge and Microsoft 365. You can also configure apps, protect apps on organizations owned and BYOD personal devices, and update apps that you deploy.

item.title: Startup Performance Report in Endpoint Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how startup performance in Microsoft Intune endpoint analytics helps analyze boot times, identify issues, and optimize device performance.

item.title: Get started with macOS endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune guide to set up and configure macOS devices from setup to creating policies and enrolling devices. You secure your corporate and organization-owned endpoints that are enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager.

item.title: Avoid policy conflicts - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to avoid policy conflicts when creating and deploying new policies.

item.title: Control AI features on Android Enterprise devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Using Microsoft Intune, you can manage and restrict AI usage on Android devices enrolled in Intune. This guide provides lists the steps in Intune to block AI apps, websites, screen-driven experiences, on-device AI services, and OEM-specific AI features. You can manage Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Samsung Galaxy AI, claude.ai, ChatGPT, and more.

item.title: Set up Microsoft Entra ID - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create and prepare your Microsoft Entra tenant for an education environment.

item.title: Configure Universal Print policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to create a Universal Print policy for Windows 10/11 client devices. The policy automatically installs printers on your managed devices.

item.title: Configure applications with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure applications with Microsoft Intune in preparation for device deployment.

item.title: Update Microsoft 365 apps with the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to update Microsoft 365 apps to the latest version, and choose how frequently Office checks for updates. See the device registry keys that are updated when an Intune policy to Office update is applied.

item.title: Update your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To support hybrid and remote workers, convert or migrate your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints. This planning guide focuses on deploying apps and updates with Intune, moving from Group Policy Objects, and using Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Deploy Microsoft Edge policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create settings using the settings catalog to configure Microsoft Edge on Windows and macOS devices. Using Microsoft Intune, you can configure group policy settings, and deploy these settings to Microsoft Edge users.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Collect Diagnostics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to collect diagnostics with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Tutorial-Get started with cloud-native Windows endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up secure cloud-native Windows endpoints that are Microsoft Entra joined, enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Use SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and assign Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Create trusted certificate profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and deploy trusted certificate profiles to deploy a trusted root certificate to managed devices in Intune. Trusted certificate profiles support use of Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) and Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Get started with iOS/iPadOS frontline worker devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using iOS and iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune. Select the best enrollment option, configure the home screen, and more.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Find lost devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Locate lost or stolen devices by using the locate device feature in Microsoft Intune. Get details on security and privacy information when using the locate device action.

item.title: Configure Recovery Lock using the settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create settings using the settings catalog to configure Recovery Lock on macOS devices. Use Recovery Lock to help protect your macOS devices against unauthorized reinstallation and wiping. Using Microsoft Intune, you can configure Recovery Lock settings, and deploy these settings to macOS devices in your organization.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Retire - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to retire device using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Common issues and resolutions with cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the known and resolutions when using cloud-native endpoints. Use user-based authentication; don't use machine authentication. Existing group policy objects might not apply.

item.title: Privacy and personal data in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn what personal data is collected and processed in Intune.

item.title: Get started with Android frontline worker devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using Android devices in Microsoft Intune. Select the best enrollment option, configure the home screen, and more.

item.title: Use a PKCS certificate profile to provision devices with certificates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificates with Microsoft Intune, work with root certificates and certificate templates, and use device configuration profiles for a PKCS Certificate.

item.title: Get started with Windows frontline worker devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using Windows devices in Microsoft Intune. Select the best enrollment option, configure the home screen, and more.

item.title: Set up enrollment for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up enrollment for macOS devices in Intune.

item.title: Manage devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of device management capabilities in Intune for Education, including remote actions, remote assistance, and inventory/reporting.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Sync - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the device sync remote action in Intune to apply policy, app, and configuration updates to managed devices.

item.title: Configure and secure devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure policies with Microsoft Intune in preparation for device deployment.

item.title: Shared iPad devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Shared iPad devices.

item.title: Common tasks and features in the settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure common features. You can create a Universal Print policy, configure Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome web browsers, and use built in settings instead of plist files for macOS devices.

item.title: Use Microsoft Intune to manage software updates for supervised iOS/iPadOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage system updates for supervised iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Common Education iPads restrictions configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about common iPads restrictions configuration used by Education organizations in Intune.

item.title: Common Education configuration overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about common configuration used by Education organizations in Intune.

item.title: Shared iOS and iPadOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Shared iOS and iPadOS devices.

item.title: Use imported PFX certificates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use imported Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificates with Microsoft Intune. Import certificates, configure certificate templates, and create an Imported PKCS Certificate profile.

item.title: Set up Apple device management in Intune for corporate-owned userless devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned userless iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Common Education iPads with no user affinity configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about common iPads with no user affinity configuration used by Education organizations in Intune.

item.title: Management functionalities for Surface devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the management capabilities offered to Surface devices, including firmware management and the Surface Management Portal.

item.title: Walkthrough-Create a settings catalog policy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This tutorial or walkthrough steps through creating and comparing an on-premises Administrative Templates (ADMX) Group Policy and Microsoft Intune cloud-based settings catalog policy. It shows similar settings in on-premises and the Intune settings catalog to create and manage policies for Office, Windows, and Microsoft Edge on Windows 10/11 client devices.

item.title: Use derived credentials for mobile devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a third-party partners derived credential as an authentication method for Intune.

item.title: Enroll a Windows device - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll a Windows device into Microsoft Intune. Follow this step-by-step evaluation guide to test device enrollment and verify it in the Intune admin center.

item.title: Sign and encrypt email using S/MIME - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use email digital certificates in Microsoft Intune to sign and encrypt emails on devices. These certificates are called S/MIME and are configured using device configuration profiles. Signing and encryption certificates use PKCS, or private certificates, and use a connector to import certificates.

item.title: Issue DigiCert PKCS certificates with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Install and configure the Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune to issue PKCS certificates from DigiCert PKI Platform to Intune-managed devices.

item.title: Configuration Policies for Intune App SDK Managed Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure policies for Intune App SDK managed apps.

item.title: Add App Configuration Policies for Managed iOS/iPadOS Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use app configuration policies to provide configuration data to an iOS/iPadOS app when it's run.

item.title: Add Built-In Apps to Mobile Devices Using Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how you can use Intune to make it easier to install built-in apps mobile devices.

item.title: Add Microsoft Edge for Windows to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about adding Microsoft Edge for Windows to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Install Microsoft 365 Apps to macOS Devices Using Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how you can use Microsoft Intune to install Microsoft 365 apps on macOS devices.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS App Provisioning Profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune gives you the tools to proactively assign a new provisioning profile to devices that have apps that are nearing expiry.

item.title: Win32 App Management in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune. This article provides an overview of the Intune Win32 app delivery and management capabilities.

item.title: Add PowerShell Scripts to Windows Devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and run PowerShell scripts, assign the script policy to Microsoft Entra groups, and use reports to monitor the scripts. See the steps to delete scripts you add on Windows devices in Microsoft Intune. Read common issues and resolutions.

item.title: Use Shell Scripts on macOS Devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, assign, monitor, and troubleshoot shell scripts for macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Step 7. Troubleshoot Microsoft Edge for Business Data Security - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 7. Troubleshoot Microsoft Edge for Business corporate data security in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add custom settings to Linux devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add Bash scripts to create a custom Linux profile in Microsoft Intune. Use the script to create, use, and control custom settings and features on Linux devices. This custom profile can then be assigned or distributed to Linux devices in your organization to create a baseline or standard.

item.title: Add custom settings for Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create a custom profile to use the OMA-URI settings for devices running Windows 10/11 client in Microsoft Intune. Use a custom profile to add custom settings.

item.title: Create macOS system and kernel extensions with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about system extensions and kernel extensions for macOS devices. In Microsoft Intune, add or create a device configuration policy that configures kernel extensions. In the extension, you can allow user override, add a team identifier, and add a bundle and team identifier.

item.title: Create an Email Device Profile for iOS/iPadOS Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create an email device profile in Microsoft Intune for iOS/iPadOS devices. Configure email settings to help users securely access organization email.

item.title: Education device enrollment with Apple Configurator and Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to bulk enroll devices with Apple Configurator.

item.title: Education device enrollment with Automated Device Enrollment and Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to automatically enroll devices through Apple School Manager with Automated Device Enrollment during Setup Assistant on iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Set up Android (AOSP) device management in Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) platform.

item.title: Get App Bundle ID - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get the app bundle ID in Microsoft Intune for Android, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Windows apps. Use the bundle ID in your app policies, device configuration profiles, enrollment policies, and compliance policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Google Chrome for Android Devices Using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune configuration policies with Google Chrome for Android devices.

item.title: Add App Configuration Policies for Managed Android Enterprise Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use app configuration policies in Microsoft Intune to supply settings when users run a Managed Google Play app.

item.title: Configure the Microsoft Managed Home Screen App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure the Microsoft Managed Home Screen app.

item.title: Manage Outlook for iOS and Android With Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune app protection and configuration policies with Outlook for iOS and Android to ensure team collaboration experiences are always accessed with safeguards in place.

item.title: Add the Company Portal for macOS App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add the macOS Company Portal app.

item.title: Add and Assign Win32 Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add, assign, and manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deploy Windows Update Packages in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to deploy a Windows update package (.msu file) in Intune.

item.title: What Is App Management in Microsoft Intune? - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about client app management capabilities by platform for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Wipe Data Using App Protection Policy Conditional Launch Actions - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to selectively wipe data using app protection policy conditional launch actions in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Data Transfer Policy Exceptions for Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create exceptions to the Intune App Protection Policy (APP) data transfer policy.

item.title: Restrict devices features using policy in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add a device configuration profile to restrict features on Android device administrator, Android Enterprise, AOSP, macOS, iOS, iPadOS, and Windows 10/11 client devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up per-app VPN for iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See the prerequisites, create a group for the virtual private network (VPN) users, add a SCEP certificate profile, configure a per-app VPN profile, and assign some apps to the VPN profile in Microsoft Intune on iOS/iPadOS devices. Also lists the steps to verify the VPN connection on the device.

item.title: Shared or multi-user Windows device settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add and use Windows 10/11 and Windows Holographic for Business devices that are shared, or used by multiple users in Microsoft Intune. See a list of all the settings and what they do on the devices, including Microsoft HoloLens. Control guest accounts, manage accounts and delete inactive accounts, allow or prevent saving to local storage, set power and sleep options, choose when updates are installed, and use devices in education environments in a device configuration profile.

item.title: Set up Android Enterprise work profile for corporate owned devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Android Enterprise work profile for corporate-owned devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: macOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll macOS devices using device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Connect Intune account to managed Google Play account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to connect your Intune account to your Managed Google Play account.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Overview of enrollment restrictions - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the enrollment restrictions available in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up web based device enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up web-based device enrollment to manage user-owned iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Admin checklist for Android software updates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidance and advice for administrators that create and manage software updated for Android devices using Microsoft Intune. See tasks and settings that can manage updates on corporate owned Android Enterprise devices.

item.title: Add Microsoft Edge to macOS Devices Using Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about adding Microsoft Edge to macOS devices using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add a Windows Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add a Windows line-of-business (LOB) app using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add an Unmanaged macOS PKG App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add an unmanaged macOS PKG app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Prepare a Win32 App to Be Uploaded to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to prepare a Win32 app to be uploaded to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Apps for GCC High and DoD Environments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about apps involving GCC High and DoD environments using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: How to Monitor App Protection Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes how to monitor app protection policies in Intune.

item.title: Step 4. Create App Configuration Policies for Microsoft Edge for Business - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 4. Create app configuration policies for Microsoft Edge for Business across Windows, Android, and iOS platforms.

item.title: Step 2. Create App Protection Policies for Microsoft Edge for Business - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 2. Create app protection policies for Microsoft Edge for Business across Windows, Android, and iOS platforms.

item.title: Step 3. Integrate Mobile Threat Defense for App Protection Policy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 3. Integrate Mobile Threat Defense signals with Microsoft Edge for Business app protection policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure device configuration profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or configure a device configuration profile in Microsoft Intune. Select the platform type, configure the settings, and add a scope tag. Learn more about applicability rules, and the policy refresh cycle times.

item.title: Domain join profile settings for Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create a domain join device configuration profile for Microsoft Entra hybrid joined devices. Use this profile to deploy on-premises Active Directory domain information to devices provisioned with Windows Autopilot and Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Upgrade Windows editions or switch S mode using Intune policy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to upgrade Windows 10/11 client devices to a different edition, or switch S mode. Administrators can use a device configuration profile to upgrade Windows client Professional to Windows client Enterprise, and switch out of S mode. See the supported upgrade paths for Windows 10/11 Pro, N Edition, Education, Cloud, Enterprise, Core, and Holographic.

item.title: Configure email settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create an email device configuration profile in Microsoft Intune, and deploy this profile to Android device administrator, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, and Windows devices. Use email profiles to configure common email settings, including a Microsoft Exchange email server. Add authentication methods to connect to corporate email on devices you manage.
